content: We can build a hybrid planning-learning system that partitions failed trajectories into two layers: high-value failures that expose unmodeled edge cases for the planner to refine its state space partitioning, and low-value random failures that are used to train the self-supervised curiosity module to avoid unproductive wandering. This turns a single pool of previously discarded failed data into a dual-purpose resource that improves both structured planning and reward-free exploration.
source_topics: Self-supervised exploration strategy improvement from failed trajectories
trigger_topic: Self-supervised exploration strategy improvement from failed trajectories
